recommended: It is recommended that the ASN.1 value NULL be used for the value portion of such bindings

mandatory: is mandatory that all implementations of the SNMP support the five PDUs
Communication among protocol entities is accomplished by the exchange of messages
A protocol entity receives messages at UDP port 161 on the host with which it is associated for all messages except for those which report traps
Messages which report traps should be received on UDP port 162 for further processing
prohibited: An implementation of this protocol need not accept messages whose length exceeds 484 octets
recommended: it is recommended that implementations support larger datagrams whenever feasible

Mandatory: is generated by a protocol entity only at the request of the SNMP application entity
Upon receipt of the Trap-PDU, the receiving protocol entity presents its contents to its SNMP application entity

Conditional constraints: If, for any object named in the variable-bindings field, the object's name does not exactly match the name of some object available for get operations in the relevant MIB view, then the receiving entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU

If, for any object named in the variable-bindings field, the object is an aggregate type (as defined in the SMI), then the receiving entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU
If the size of the GetResponse-PDU generated as described below would exceed a local limitation, then the receiving entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU

If, for any object named in the variable-bindings field, the value of the object cannot be retrieved for reasons not covered by any of the foregoing rules, then the receiving entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU
If none of the foregoing rules apply, then the receiving protocol entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU

Mandatory: is generated by a protocol entity only at the request of its SNMP application entity
the receiving protocol entity responds according to any applicable rule in the list below
the receiving entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU
the receiving entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU
the receiving entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU
the receiving protocol entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU
Prohibited: cannot be retrieved for reasons not covered by any of the foregoing rules
Conditional constraints: If, for any object name in the variable-bindings field, that object name does not lexicographically precede the name of some object available for get operations in the relevant MIB view, then the receiving entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU
If the size of the GetResponse-PDU generated as described below would exceed a local limitation, then the receiving entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU

If, for any object named in the variable-bindings field, the value of the lexicographical successor to the named object cannot be retrieved for reasons not covered by any of the foregoing rules, then the receiving entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU
If none of the foregoing rules apply, then the receiving protocol entity sends to the originator of the received message the GetResponse-PDU
